ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for jasmine green tea with lemon and coconut jelly specifically; closest parent 'green tea beverage' (FOODON_03311737) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Tea, green) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for flavoured jasmine green tea with coconut jelly toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 'Green tea (substance)' is the closest parent; no specific concept exists for this composite beverage with coconut jelly.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one 400 ml cup of lemon jasmine green tea with coconut jelly, as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea shops.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central: Tea, green, brewed (NDB 14355) and Coconut water jelly / nata de coco (USDA SR Legacy) with lemon juice component
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for lemon jasmine green tea with coconut jelly in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component: brewed jasmine green tea (~1 kcal/100 ml, negligible macros), lemon juice contribution (~3 kcal/100 ml), sugar syrup (variable, estimated 6–8 g sugar/100 ml for a lightly sweetened version), and coconut jelly/nata de coco (~60 kcal/100 g, ~15 g carbohydrate). The composite per-100 g estimate reflects a diluted sweetened tea with a moderate proportion of coconut jelly. Energy estimated at approximately 35 kcal/100 g for the combined beverage. Protein and fat are effectively zero from tea and lemon; coconut jelly contributes negligible protein and fat. Sugar is the dominant carbohydrate source from added syrup and coconut jelly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sucrose and sugar-sweetened beverages reference; green tea GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lemon jasmine green tea with coconut jelly. GI estimated at 52 based on: (1) plain green tea has negligible GI (set to 1 per convention for zero-carb beverages); (2) the dominant glycemic driver is added sugar syrup (sucrose GI ~65) and coconut jelly (nata de coco, low GI ~40–45 per fermented coconut product analogy); (3) the blended GI weighted by carbohydrate contribution yields an estimated GI of approximately 52, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 34.0) / 100 = 17.7, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 8.5 × 4 = 34.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg/100 g); green tea polyphenols may support mild vasodilation
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 g, this beverage poses minimal sodium load for hypertensive individuals; however, added sugar content should be monitored as high sugar intake can indirectly elevate blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar syrup contributes approximately 7.5 g sugar per 100 g; estimated GI of 52 (low) but glycemic load per full serving is moderate at ~17.7
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this beverage due to the added sugar content; requesting reduced or no sugar syrup and limiting portion size to half a cup can substantially lower the glycemic load to a clinically acceptable level.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; green tea catechins (EGCG) have documented cardioprotective antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage is low in saturated fat and cholesterol, and jasmine green tea provides catechins with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may support cardiovascular health; however, regular consumption of the sweetened version should be moderated to avoid excess sugar intake contributing to dyslipidaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Jasmine Green Tea with Coconut Jelly is a chilled sweetened tea beverage popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, commonly sold at bubble tea and specialty tea shops. It is made by brewing jasmine-scented green tea leaves, chilling the brew, adding freshly squeezed or concentrated lemon juice for tartness, sweetening with sugar syrup to taste, and serving over ice with chewy translucent coconut jelly pieces (nata de coco) as a topping. The flavour profile is floral and fragrant from the jasmine green tea, bright and citrusy from the lemon, and mildly sweet with a refreshing finish. The coconut jelly adds a pleasant chewy texture contrast. Nutritionally, the beverage is low in energy (approximately 35 kcal per 100 g), contains negligible protein and fat, and derives most of its calories from added sugar. The green tea base provides polyphenols including catechins (notably EGCG) which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Lemon juice contributes a small amount of vitamin C. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 52 (low), though the glycemic load per full 400 ml serving is moderate (~17.7) due to the sugar content. This beverage is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, is dairy-free and gluten-free, and is generally considered halal. Clinically, individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome should request reduced sugar versions to lower glycemic impact. The beverage is low in sodium and saturated fat, making it broadly compatible with heart-healthy and hypertension-conscious dietary patterns when consumed in moderation.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific composite beverage exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA FoodData Central data for brewed green tea, lemon juice, sugar syrup, and nata de coco. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database references for sucrose and fermented coconut products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are well-established and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no single approved-source entry covers this exact composite beverage. Values should be updated if HPB Singapore Foodo or a peer-reviewed compositional study publishes data for this specific product type. 2. Protein_g (0.1) and fat_g (0.1) are trace values from lemon juice and coconut jelly components; they are not zero — confirmed trace per USDA FoodData Central component data. 3. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 — confirmed zero or naturally zero for this beverage type as the fat content is below detection threshold in the tea and lemon components, and nata de coco contains negligible fat. 4. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for brewed green tea, lemon juice, or nata de coco at the level of precision required for this composite beverage estimate; value cannot be defensibly derived. 6. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 7. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 8. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 9. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 10. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this plant-based beverage; vitamin B12 is not present in plant-derived ingredients and is naturally zero, but -1 is used here as the source does not explicitly report a confirmed zero for the composite. 11. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the diluted contribution from tea and lemon juice cannot be defensibly estimated. 12. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this beverage; vitamin D is not present in meaningful quantities in plant-based tea beverages and is not reported by the source. 13. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 14. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the diluted contribution from tea and nata de coco cannot be defensibly estimated. 15. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the diluted contribution from tea and nata de coco cannot be defensibly estimated. 16. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; the contribution from diluted tea and lemon juice is below reliable estimation threshold. 17. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this composite beverage; selenium content of brewed tea and lemon juice is not reliably reported at the dilution level of this beverage. 18. GI set to 52 (low, ≤55) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database references; glycemic load per 400 g serving = 17.7. 19. The food_name has been normalised from the input 'Lemon Premium Jasmine Green Tea with Coco Jelly' to the canonical form 'Lemon Jasmine Green Tea with Coconut Jelly'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases. 20. This beverage is classified as Chinese cuisine reflecting the jasmine green tea tradition, served in a Singaporean bubble tea context; multilingual names cover all five mandatory Singaporean cuisine languages (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). 21. glycemic_index set to 52 (low) based on weighted component GI estimate; gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule.
